producers.philosophy: The family that invented Cava in 1872 later left the DO to create Conca del Riu Anoia, championing terroir-specific sparkling wines. Biodynamic farming and extended lees aging create complex, distinctive sparklers.

wines.name: De Nit Rosé
wines.notes: Elegant rosé sparkling with strawberry, citrus, and fine mousse.

wines.name: Textures de Pedra
wines.notes: Complex blanc de noirs sparkling with toasty, mineral character.

producers.awards: The family that invented Cava; left the DO to champion terroir.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Bodega Marañones
producers.philosophy: Fernando García rescues old Garnacha vineyards in the Sierra de Gredos, crafting elegant, Burgundy-inflected reds at altitude. A leader of Spain's new wave of terroir-driven winemaking.

wines.name: Marañones
wines.notes: Elegant and perfumed with bright red fruit, wild herbs, and fine, silky tannins.

wines.name: 30,000 Maravedíes
wines.notes: Old-vine Garnacha of exceptional complexity and mineral depth.

producers.awards: Leading voice of the Sierra de Gredos Garnacha movement.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Bodegas El Nido
producers.philosophy: A collaboration between Gil Family Estates and Chris Ringland (Australia), producing intense, concentrated wines from old-vine Monastrell in Jumilla. El Nido and Clio are cult-status wines.

wines.name: El Nido
wines.notes: Massively concentrated with dark fruit, chocolate, smoke, and powerful tannins.

wines.name: Clio
wines.notes: More accessible but still intensely rich with dark plum, spice, and firm tannins.

producers.awards: Cult-status Jumilla wines from old-vine Monastrell.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Comando G
producers.philosophy: Fernando García and Dani Landi craft single-vineyard Garnachas from old vines in the Sierra de Gredos mountains. Burgundian in ambition, these are among Spain's most elegant and sought-after wines.

wines.name: La Bruja de Rozas
wines.notes: Fresh and vibrant with red cherry, wild herbs, and gentle tannins.

wines.name: Rumbo al Norte
wines.notes: Single-vineyard old-vine Garnacha of haunting elegance and mineral complexity.

producers.awards: At the forefront of Spain's Garnacha renaissance in Sierra de Gredos.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.sommelierNote: Daniel Landi and Fernando García make pure, high-altitude Garnacha from old vines in the Sierra de Gredos — Spain's most exciting emerging region.

producers.name: Bodegas Frontonio
producers.philosophy: A project recovering old bush-vine Garnacha in the high-altitude Valdejalón region of Aragón. Francisco Latasa crafts transparent, mineral wines that showcase forgotten terroirs.

wines.name: Telescópica
wines.notes: Pure and elegant with bright red fruit, wild herbs, and fine tannins.

wines.name: Supersónico
wines.notes: An everyday Garnacha of surprising purity and freshness.

producers.awards: Outstanding value old-vine Garnacha from high-altitude Aragón.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Envínate
producers.philosophy: Four friends making wines across Spain's most extreme terroirs: volcanic Canary Islands, steep Ribeira Sacra, and mountain Almansa. Minimal intervention reveals extraordinary terroir diversity.

wines.name: Migan Tenerife
wines.notes: Volcanic and wild with red fruit, smoke, ash, and extraordinary mineral intensity.

wines.name: Lousas Viñas de Aldea Ribeira Sacra
wines.notes: Elegant and fresh with dark cherry, slate, and violet from steep terraces.

producers.awards: Among Spain's most exciting producers, spanning extreme terroirs.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.sommelierNote: Four friends making wine in the Canary Islands, Ribeira Sacra, and beyond — volcanic, radical, site-specific wines from pre-phylloxera vines.

producers.name: Ossian Vides y Vinos
producers.philosophy: Rescuing pre-phylloxera, ungrafted Verdejo bush vines up to 200 years old on sandy soils. These extraordinary old vines produce wines of a complexity and depth unimaginable for the Rueda region.

wines.name: Ossian
wines.notes: Profound old-vine Verdejo with stone fruit, wild herbs, mineral depth, and remarkable complexity.

wines.name: Capitel
wines.notes: The top selection from the oldest vines, of extraordinary concentration.

producers.awards: 200-year-old ungrafted Verdejo vines produce extraordinary wines.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Scarecrow
producers.philosophy: Scarecrow is crafted from the legendary J.J. Cohn estate vineyard in Rutherford, farmed organically and with meticulous attention. The wine channels the Rutherford dust terroir with extraordinary concentration and finesse.

wines.name: Scarecrow Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Opulent and layered with blackberry, cassis, cocoa, and dried herbs over a velvety tannic frame.

wines.name: M. Etain
wines.notes: The second label offering approachable dark fruit and spice with refined structure.

producers.awards: Multiple 100-point scores from Robert Parker; consistently ranked among Napa's finest.

buyingGuide.note: Mailing list only (long waitlist). Auction: Hart Davis Hart.

producers.name: Dalla Valle
producers.philosophy: Founded by Gustav and Naoko Dalla Valle on a steep hillside vineyard above Oakville, the estate produces intensely concentrated wines from low-yielding vines. Andy Erickson and Michel Rolland have both contributed to its acclaimed winemaking legacy.

wines.name: Dalla Valle Maya
wines.notes: A Bordeaux-style blend of extraordinary depth with black fruit, graphite, and sweet oak.

wines.name: Dalla Valle Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Rich and structured with dark cherry, plum, and mineral notes framed by firm tannins.

producers.awards: Maya received a perfect 100 from Parker for the 1992 and 1993 vintages.

buyingGuide.note: Mailing list allocation. Secondary market retailers.

producers.name: Colgin Cellars
producers.philosophy: Ann Colgin founded this estate with a vision to produce wines of exceptional purity and precision from Napa's prized Pritchard Hill. Winemaking balances power with elegance through meticulous sorting and gentle extraction.

wines.name: Colgin IX Estate
wines.notes: A Bordeaux-style blend with layers of blackcurrant, violet, graphite, and espresso in a seamless, polished frame.

wines.name: Colgin Tychson Hill
wines.notes: Intense and pure with dark berry, wild sage, and fine-grained tannins from a historic vineyard site.

producers.awards: Seven perfect 100-point scores from Robert Parker; one of Napa's most celebrated estates.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Bryant Family Vineyard
producers.philosophy: Perched high on Pritchard Hill, Bryant Family farms its steep terraced vineyard with organic and biodynamic practices. The estate's singular focus is producing a powerful yet refined Cabernet that reflects its extraordinary mountain terroir.

wines.name: Bryant Family Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Massively concentrated with blackberry, cassis, dark chocolate, and crushed stone, framed by ripe yet firm tannins.

wines.name: Bryant Family DB4
wines.notes: A more approachable blend with vibrant dark fruit, spice, and supple texture.

producers.awards: 100-point debut vintage from Parker in 1992; perennial scores above 95 points.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Eisele Vineyard
producers.philosophy: Now owned by Chateau Latour's Artemis Group, Eisele Vineyard is one of Napa's most historic and revered sites with vines dating back over a century. The estate practices certified organic farming and produces wines of remarkable purity and longevity.

wines.name: Eisele Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Complex and age-worthy with cassis, iron, dried herbs, and graphite, displaying incredible minerality.

wines.name: Eisele Vineyard Altagracia
wines.notes: The second wine with polished red and dark fruit, cedar, and fine tannins.

producers.awards: Formerly Araujo Estate; multiple perfect scores and recognized as a Napa Grand Cru-level site.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Chateau Montelena
producers.philosophy: Famous for its role in the 1976 Judgment of Paris, Chateau Montelena proved that California wines could rival the best of France. The estate continues to produce classically structured wines with restrained winemaking and no malolactic fermentation on its Chardonnay.

wines.name: Chateau Montelena Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Firmly structured with dark fruit, dried herbs, cedar, and dusty tannins in a classic Calistoga style.

wines.name: Chateau Montelena Chardonnay
wines.notes: Crisp and mineral-driven with citrus, green apple, and restrained oak influence.

producers.awards: Won the 1976 Judgment of Paris white wine tasting; a cornerstone of Napa Valley history.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.sommelierNote: Their 1973 Chardonnay won the Judgment of Paris white wine tasting — classic, restrained Napa wines that prioritize balance over power.

producers.name: Clos du Val
producers.philosophy: Founded by French winemaker Bernard Portet, Clos du Val brought a Bordeaux sensibility to Stags Leap District winemaking. The estate emphasizes balance and ageability over sheer power, crafting wines of restrained elegance.

wines.name: Clos du Val Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Medium-bodied and elegant with red currant, cedar, and earthy notes in a classic, age-worthy style.

wines.name: Clos du Val Hirondelle Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Rich and layered with dark fruit, spice, and polished tannins from a prized Stags Leap site.

producers.awards: Placed among top wines in the 1986 rematch of the Judgment of Paris.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Freemark Abbey
producers.philosophy: One of Napa's oldest continuously operating wineries, Freemark Abbey has been a benchmark for single-vineyard Cabernet since the 1960s. The estate champions site-specific winemaking with minimal intervention.

wines.name: Freemark Abbey Bosché Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Complex and structured with dark berry, tobacco, and mineral tones from one of Napa's first vineyard-designated wines.

wines.name: Freemark Abbey Sycamore Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Rich and full-bodied with ripe plum, cassis, and cedary oak.

producers.awards: Bosché Vineyard was among the first vineyard-designated Cabernets in Napa Valley history.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Spring Mountain Vineyard
producers.philosophy: Situated on 845 acres of forested mountainside, Spring Mountain Vineyard farms organically across multiple distinct vineyard blocks. The estate leverages dramatic elevation changes and volcanic soils to produce wines of great complexity.

wines.name: Spring Mountain Vineyard Elivette
wines.notes: A Bordeaux-style blend of great depth with cassis, dark chocolate, and mountain minerality.

wines.name: Spring Mountain Vineyard Sauvignon Blanc
wines.notes: Bright and textured with Meyer lemon, stone fruit, and crisp acidity.

producers.awards: Consistent 90+ scores; Elivette flagship routinely rated among top Spring Mountain wines.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Newton Vineyard
producers.philosophy: Peter and Su Hua Newton established this estate on the steep slopes of Spring Mountain with a commitment to unfiltered, naturally fermented wines. Now owned by LVMH, Newton continues to emphasize mountain-grown fruit and minimal intervention.

wines.name: Newton Unfiltered Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Rich and layered with dark plum, espresso, and baking spice, bottled without filtration for full texture.

wines.name: Newton Unfiltered Chardonnay
wines.notes: Creamy and complex with baked apple, hazelnut, and a long mineral finish.

producers.awards: Pioneer of unfiltered wine in Napa; consistently well-reviewed across the portfolio.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Chappellet
producers.philosophy: Donn and Molly Chappellet were pioneers on Pritchard Hill, planting at elevations above the fog line to produce intensely flavored mountain Cabernet. The family continues to farm sustainably with deep respect for their iconic terroir.

wines.name: Chappellet Pritchard Hill Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Powerful and complex with blackberry, iron, sage, and muscular tannins from volcanic mountain soils.

wines.name: Chappellet Signature Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Approachable yet structured with dark cherry, cocoa, and fine-grained tannins.

producers.awards: Pritchard Hill bottling consistently scores 95-100; one of Napa's original mountain estates.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Realm Cellars
producers.philosophy: Realm sources fruit from some of Napa's most prized vineyards including Beckstoffer To Kalon and Dr. Crane. Winemaker Benoit Touquette crafts wines that are opulent yet structured with a focus on site expression.

wines.name: Realm The Bard
wines.notes: A multi-vineyard blend of great depth with cassis, dark chocolate, espresso, and velvety tannins.

wines.name: Realm Beckstoffer To Kalon
wines.notes: Intense and layered with blackberry, graphite, and crushed stone from the legendary vineyard site.

producers.awards: Multiple 98-100 point scores from Parker and Galloni; meteoric rise to cult status.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.sommelierNote: Benoit Touquette (formerly of Screaming Eagle) makes cult Napa Cabernet from the valley's most famous vineyard sources.

producers.name: Dana Estates
producers.philosophy: Korean-born Hi Sang Lee created Dana Estates with a singular vision of producing Napa's finest Cabernet from distinct mountain vineyard sites. Biodynamic farming and fanatical attention to detail define every aspect of production.

wines.name: Dana Estates Lotus Vineyard
wines.notes: Profoundly concentrated with blackberry, exotic spice, violet, and incredibly polished tannins.

wines.name: Dana Estates Hershey Vineyard
wines.notes: Rich and powerful with dark plum, chocolate, and crushed rock from volcanic soils.

producers.awards: Multiple perfect 100-point scores; one of the fastest-rising cult wineries in Napa.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Hourglass
producers.philosophy: Named for the hourglass shape of the original Blueline Vineyard, Hourglass produces site-driven wines from organically farmed estate vineyards. The focus is on pure, concentrated expressions of Napa Cabernet with a balance of power and finesse.

wines.name: Hourglass Blueline Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Dense and complex with blackcurrant, sage, dark earth, and fine-grained tannins.

wines.name: Hourglass HG III
wines.notes: A multi-vineyard blend with ripe dark fruit, mocha, and approachable structure.

producers.awards: Consistently rated 94-98 points; recognized as a top Calistoga estate.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: PlumpJack Winery
producers.philosophy: Co-founded by Gavin Newsom, PlumpJack was a pioneer in offering reserve wines with screwcap closures, challenging convention. The estate produces rich, approachable Cabernets from prime Oakville fruit.

wines.name: PlumpJack Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Luxurious and full-bodied with blackberry, cassis, vanilla, and plush tannins.

wines.name: PlumpJack Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Rich and ripe with dark cherry, mocha, and spice in a generous, approachable style.

producers.awards: Reserve regularly scores 95+ points; a trailblazer for screwcap in premium wine.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Trefethen Family Vineyards
producers.philosophy: The Trefethen family farms one of Napa's largest contiguous estate vineyards sustainably in the cool Oak Knoll District. Their wines emphasize freshness and varietal purity over heavy oak or extraction.

wines.name: Trefethen Halo Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Elegant and layered with dark cherry, dried herb, and a long mineral-driven finish.

wines.name: Trefethen Estate Chardonnay
wines.notes: Crisp and balanced with green apple, citrus, and subtle oak complexity.

producers.awards: Won the 1979 Gault-Millau Wine Olympiad in Paris for its Chardonnay.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Frog's Leap
producers.philosophy: John Williams has championed organic and dry-farmed viticulture in Napa Valley for over four decades. Frog's Leap produces balanced, food-friendly wines that reflect Rutherford terroir without excessive ripeness.

wines.name: Frog's Leap Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Medium-bodied and elegant with red currant, dried herbs, and dusty Rutherford tannins.

wines.name: Frog's Leap Sauvignon Blanc
wines.notes: Bright and lively with grapefruit, lime zest, and crisp acidity.

producers.awards: A pioneer of organic farming in Napa; consistently praised for outstanding value.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Grgich Hills Estate
producers.philosophy: Miljenko 'Mike' Grgich, the winemaker behind the Judgment of Paris-winning Chardonnay, cofounded this estate with Austin Hills. The winery farms biodynamically and crafts wines of purity and balance true to the Grgich tradition.

wines.name: Grgich Hills Chardonnay
wines.notes: Elegant and precise with citrus, pear, and subtle minerality; made in the classic Grgich style.

wines.name: Grgich Hills Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Structured and earthy with dark fruit, cedar, and firm tannins.

producers.awards: Mike Grgich crafted the 1973 Chateau Montelena Chardonnay that won the 1976 Judgment of Paris.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: ZD Wines
producers.philosophy: The deLeuze family has produced estate wines in Napa for over five decades with a focus on rich, full-flavored expressions. ZD is known for its generous, barrel-aged Chardonnay and structured Rutherford Cabernet.

wines.name: ZD Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Full-bodied and plush with black cherry, chocolate, and spice framed by supple tannins.

wines.name: ZD Chardonnay
wines.notes: Rich and creamy with baked apple, vanilla, and a buttery, lingering finish.

producers.awards: Consistent 90+ scores across the portfolio for decades; a reliable Napa name.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: HALL Wines
producers.philosophy: Kathryn Walt Hall, former U.S. Ambassador to Austria, established HALL with a commitment to organic viticulture and sustainable architecture. The estate produces premium Cabernets from benchmark Napa vineyard sites including Sacrashe.

wines.name: HALL Kathryn Hall Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Opulent and complex with dark berry, espresso, and cedar in a full-bodied, age-worthy frame.

wines.name: HALL Sauvignon Blanc
wines.notes: Vibrant and aromatic with citrus, melon, and a crisp, refreshing finish.

producers.awards: Kathryn Hall bottling regularly scores 95+ points; certified organic estate.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Pine Ridge Vineyards
producers.philosophy: Pine Ridge sources from five distinct Napa Valley appellations, crafting vineyard-designated wines that showcase each terroir. The estate is committed to sustainable farming and precise, terroir-expressive winemaking.

wines.name: Pine Ridge Stags Leap District Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Velvety and aromatic with dark cherry, black olive, and iron-tinged minerality.

wines.name: Pine Ridge Chenin Blanc + Viognier
wines.notes: Floral and off-dry with peach, honeysuckle, and bright acidity.

producers.awards: Stags Leap District bottling consistently rated 90-94 points.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Robert Foley Vineyards
producers.philosophy: Bob Foley, former winemaker at Pride Mountain, started his own label focused on powerful mountain-grown reds. He is known for his work with unusual varieties like Charbono alongside classic Howell Mountain Cabernet.

wines.name: Robert Foley Claret
wines.notes: A Bordeaux-style blend with dark fruit, chocolate, and firm structure from mountain fruit.

wines.name: Robert Foley Charbono
wines.notes: Rare and rustic with dark berry, tar, and earthy complexity from old Calistoga vines.

producers.awards: Claret regularly scores 92-96 points; one of the few producers of varietal Charbono.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: TOR Kenward Family Wines
producers.philosophy: Tor Kenward, the former VP of Beringer, launched his own label to produce small-lot, vineyard-designated wines from Napa's top sites. Each bottling is crafted to capture the unique expression of individual vineyard blocks.

wines.name: TOR Beckstoffer To Kalon Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Powerful and refined with cassis, graphite, espresso, and remarkably polished tannins.

wines.name: TOR Beresini Vineyard Chardonnay
wines.notes: Rich and textured with citrus oil, hazelnut, and subtle smoky minerality.

producers.awards: To Kalon bottling regularly scores 96-100 points from major critics.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Favia Wines
producers.philosophy: Andy Erickson and Annie Favia, one of Napa's most celebrated winemaker-viticulturist couples, produce tiny quantities from their own vineyard and select sites. Their wines emphasize site and vintage expression with natural winemaking techniques.

wines.name: Favia Cerro Sur
wines.notes: Complex and layered with dark fruit, garrigue, and stony minerality from Coombsville.

wines.name: Favia Rompecabezas
wines.notes: Savory and perfumed with blackberry, smoked meat, and cracked pepper.

producers.awards: Highly rated by Galloni and Pepe; one of Napa's most sought-after micro-producers.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Provenance Vineyards
producers.philosophy: Provenance is dedicated to showcasing the distinct character of Rutherford and other premier Napa Valley appellations. The winemaking emphasizes approachable, food-friendly styles that reflect their vineyard sources.

wines.name: Provenance Rutherford Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Medium-bodied with dark cherry, dried herb, and dusty tannins characteristic of Rutherford.

wines.name: Provenance Merlot
wines.notes: Plush and approachable with plum, chocolate, and gentle spice.

producers.awards: Consistently well-reviewed as a quality Napa producer at accessible pricing.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Lewis Cellars
producers.philosophy: Randy Lewis, a former race car driver, founded Lewis Cellars with a focus on rich, intensely flavored wines from top Napa vineyards. The winemaking favors ripe fruit, generous oak, and full-throttle concentration.

wines.name: Lewis Cellars Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Bold and hedonistic with blackberry, dark chocolate, vanilla, and plush, enveloping tannins.

wines.name: Lewis Cellars Napa Valley Chardonnay
wines.notes: Rich and creamy with tropical fruit, toasted oak, and a buttery finish.

producers.awards: Reserve bottling regularly scores 95-98 points from major publications.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: BOND
producers.philosophy: Created by Bill Harlan as a portfolio of single-vineyard Cabernets from Napa's finest hillside sites, BOND explores the concept of Napa Grand Crus. Each vineyard is farmed to exacting standards and vinified by winemaker Cory Empting.

wines.name: BOND Vecina
wines.notes: Muscular and structured with blackberry, iron, crushed rock, and firm, age-worthy tannins.

wines.name: BOND Quella
wines.notes: Elegant and perfumed with red and dark fruit, spice, and silky texture.

producers.awards: Multiple perfect scores across vineyards; regarded as Napa's Grand Cru project.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Meteor Vineyard
producers.philosophy: Located on an ancient meteorite impact site in Coombsville, this small estate produces a single Bordeaux blend from volcanic soils. Winemaker Bill and Lily Leigon craft wines that express this unique terroir with precision.

wines.name: Meteor Special Family Reserve
wines.notes: Deep and complex with dark fruit, crushed stone, espresso, and refined tannins from volcanic soils.

wines.name: Perseid
wines.notes: The second wine offering approachable dark fruit and spice with supple structure.

producers.awards: Consistently rated 94-98 points; one of Coombsville's most acclaimed estates.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Nickel & Nickel
producers.philosophy: Sister winery to Far Niente, Nickel & Nickel is devoted entirely to single-vineyard, single-varietal wines from Napa's top appellations. Each bottling is meant to be a pure expression of its vineyard source.

wines.name: Nickel & Nickel John C. Sullenger Vineyard Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Concentrated and structured with dark fruit, tobacco, and firm Oakville tannins.

wines.name: Nickel & Nickel Truchard Vineyard Chardonnay
wines.notes: Bright and focused with citrus, stone fruit, and crisp minerality.

producers.awards: Consistently rated 90-96 across vineyard designations.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Staglin Family Vineyard
producers.philosophy: The Staglin family farms organically in the heart of Rutherford, producing estate wines that express the famed Rutherford Bench terroir. Their annual Music Festival for Mental Health is one of Napa's premier philanthropic events.

wines.name: Staglin Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Powerful and polished with black cherry, sage, cocoa, and dusty Rutherford tannins.

wines.name: Staglin Salus Estate Chardonnay
wines.notes: Richly textured with citrus, pear, and elegant oak spice.

producers.awards: Estate Cabernet regularly scores 95-99; one of Rutherford's top estates.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Vineyard 29
producers.philosophy: Located adjacent to the Grace Family Vineyard, Vineyard 29 produces exceptionally concentrated estate Cabernet from volcanic benchland soils. Winemaker Keith Emerson crafts wines of power and polish from this exceptional site.

wines.name: Vineyard 29 Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Dense and opulent with blackberry, cassis, espresso, and lavish, velvety tannins.

wines.name: Vineyard 29 Aida Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Powerful mountain fruit with dark plum, mineral, and structured tannins.

producers.awards: Multiple 97-100 scores from Parker; one of Napa's most exclusive Cabernet producers.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Odette Estate
producers.philosophy: Part of the PlumpJack family of wineries, Odette Estate farms organically in the Stags Leap District with a focus on elegant, terroir-driven Cabernet. Winemaker Jeff Ames emphasizes finesse and aromatic complexity.

wines.name: Odette Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Supple and elegant with dark cherry, violet, iron, and silky Stags Leap tannins.

wines.name: Odette Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Concentrated and layered with cassis, graphite, and refined structure.

producers.awards: Reserve bottling scored 100 points from Robert Parker in its early vintages.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Ovid Napa Valley
producers.philosophy: Perched atop Pritchard Hill with panoramic views, Ovid produces small-lot Bordeaux-style blends from its organically farmed estate. Winemaker Austin Peterson crafts wines that balance power with a sense of place.

wines.name: Ovid Napa Valley Red
wines.notes: A compelling blend with dark fruit, graphite, wild herbs, and muscular yet refined tannins.

wines.name: Ovid Hexameter
wines.notes: Structured and mineral-driven with blackberry, iron, and crushed volcanic stone.

producers.awards: Regularly scores 96-100 points; one of Pritchard Hill's most celebrated estates.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Lokoya
producers.philosophy: Lokoya produces four mountain-designated Cabernets from Napa's most extreme high-elevation sites including Mount Veeder, Howell Mountain, Diamond Mountain, and Spring Mountain. Each wine is a pure expression of mountain terroir.

wines.name: Lokoya Mount Veeder Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Massively concentrated with wild blackberry, sage, iron, and powerful mountain tannins.

wines.name: Lokoya Howell Mountain Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Dense and structured with dark plum, crushed rock, and extraordinary depth.

producers.awards: Multiple perfect 100-point scores across all four mountain designations.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: CADE Estate
producers.philosophy: Part of the PlumpJack group, CADE is one of Napa's few LEED Gold-certified wineries, built sustainably on Howell Mountain. The estate produces organic mountain Cabernet with a focus on environmental stewardship.

wines.name: CADE Estate Howell Mountain Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Powerful and mineral-driven with dark berry, sage, graphite, and firm mountain tannins.

wines.name: CADE Sauvignon Blanc
wines.notes: Crisp and aromatic with citrus, tropical fruit, and bright acidity.

producers.awards: Estate Cabernet regularly scores 94-98; praised for its sustainability commitment.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Morlet Family Vineyards
producers.philosophy: French-born winemaker Luc Morlet brings a Burgundian sensibility to his Napa estate, producing small-lot wines of extraordinary precision and complexity. Each bottling is named for a family member and crafted with meticulous care.

wines.name: Morlet Passionnement Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Opulent and layered with blackberry, dark chocolate, violet, and seamless, polished tannins.

wines.name: Morlet Ma Princesse Chardonnay
wines.notes: Burgundian in style with lemon curd, hazelnut, and a long, mineral finish.

producers.awards: Multiple perfect 100-point scores; one of Napa's most acclaimed boutique producers.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Blankiet Estate
producers.philosophy: Claude Blankiet's hillside estate in the Yountville hills produces richly concentrated Bordeaux-style blends under the guidance of consultant Michel Rolland. The estate's steep, rocky vineyards yield tiny quantities of profoundly concentrated fruit.

wines.name: Blankiet Paradise Hills Vineyard
wines.notes: Lush and opulent with blackberry, cassis, mocha, and velvety, enveloping tannins.

wines.name: Blankiet Rive Droite
wines.notes: A Right Bank-inspired blend with plum, truffle, and silky texture.

producers.awards: Consistently scored 95-99 points by Parker and Galloni.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Larkmead Vineyards
producers.philosophy: One of Napa's oldest continuously operating estates, Larkmead is farmed organically and produces wines that reflect over a century of viticultural heritage. Winemaker Dan Petroski also produces the acclaimed Massican white wines here.

wines.name: Larkmead Solari Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Complex and structured with dark cherry, sage, graphite, and muscular tannins from old-vine Calistoga fruit.

wines.name: Larkmead Firebelle
wines.notes: A Bordeaux-style blend with dark fruit, dried herbs, and firm, elegant structure.

producers.awards: Solari regularly scores 95-98; one of Napa's most historically significant estates.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Ghost Block Estate
producers.philosophy: Ghost Block takes its name from a section of Oakville vineyard that George Yount planted in the 1800s but was thought lost. The estate now produces rich Oakville Cabernet from this historic benchland site.

wines.name: Ghost Block Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Full-bodied and plush with dark berry, cassis, chocolate, and supple Oakville tannins.

wines.name: Ghost Block Sauvignon Blanc
wines.notes: Bright and refreshing with grapefruit, melon, and crisp acidity.

producers.awards: Consistent 91-94 scores; excellent value for Oakville Cabernet.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Revana Family Vineyard
producers.philosophy: Dr. Madaiah Revana planted his vineyard in the heart of St. Helena with the singular goal of producing world-class Cabernet. Winemaker Thomas Rivers Brown crafts deeply concentrated wines from this organically farmed estate.

wines.name: Revana Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Rich and layered with blackberry, espresso, dark chocolate, and polished tannins.

wines.name: Revana Terroir Series Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Approachable with bright dark fruit, cedar, and supple structure.

producers.awards: Estate bottling regularly scores 94-98 from major critics.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Cliff Lede Vineyards
producers.philosophy: Canadian-born Cliff Lede combined his passions for wine and rock music, naming vineyard blocks after classic rock songs. The estate produces refined Stags Leap District Cabernet from sustainably farmed vineyards.

wines.name: Cliff Lede Poetry Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Elegant and complex with dark cherry, violet, graphite, and silky Stags Leap tannins.

wines.name: Cliff Lede Sauvignon Blanc
wines.notes: Vibrant and aromatic with citrus, white peach, and crisp minerality.

producers.awards: Poetry bottling consistently scores 94-97 points.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Tudal Winery
producers.philosophy: The Tudal family has been farming Cabernet in St. Helena for five decades, producing honest, terroir-driven wines without pretense. The small estate remains family-owned and focused on value-driven quality.

wines.name: Tudal Estate Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Classic and well-structured with dark cherry, cedar, and earthy Napa character.

wines.name: Tudal Tractor Shed Red
wines.notes: A fruit-forward blend with ripe berry, spice, and soft tannins.

producers.awards: A beloved Napa insider's pick for honest, well-priced estate Cabernet.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Outpost Wines
producers.philosophy: Frank and Kathy Dotzler farm their Howell Mountain estate at extreme elevation, producing powerful reds from volcanic soils. Thomas Rivers Brown makes the wines with a focus on site expression and intense mountain character.

wines.name: Outpost Howell Mountain Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Dense and structured with dark fruit, crushed rock, sage, and powerful tannins.

wines.name: Outpost Howell Mountain Zinfandel
wines.notes: Bold and spicy with ripe blackberry, pepper, and earthy depth.

producers.awards: Cabernet regularly scores 94-97; one of Howell Mountain's top estates.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Maybach Family Vineyards
producers.philosophy: Named for the pioneering American family that settled in Napa Valley, Maybach focuses on single-vineyard Cabernets from Beckstoffer heritage sites. Winemaker Andy Erickson crafts wines of opulence and precision.

wines.name: Maybach Amoenus Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Rich and velvety with blackberry, dark chocolate, and espresso in a lavish, full-bodied style.

wines.name: Maybach Materium Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Powerful and concentrated from Beckstoffer Dr. Crane vineyard with cassis and crushed stone.

producers.awards: Multiple 97-100 scores; quickly ascending among Napa's most sought-after labels.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Carlisle Winery
producers.philosophy: Mike Officer is a champion of old-vine heritage vineyards throughout Sonoma and beyond, producing single-vineyard wines from gnarly, ancient vines. His winemaking is minimalist, letting the character of century-old plantings shine through.

wines.name: Carlisle Papera Ranch Zinfandel
wines.notes: Rich and complex from old vines with blackberry, earth, pepper, and remarkable depth.

wines.name: Carlisle Montafi Ranch Syrah
wines.notes: Savory and peppery with dark fruit, smoked meat, and olive tapenade.

producers.awards: Widely praised for preserving California's old-vine heritage; consistently rated 92-96.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Kutch Wines
producers.philosophy: Jamie Kutch transitioned from Wall Street to pursue a passion for whole-cluster, terroir-driven Pinot Noir. His wines are crafted with minimal intervention and emphasize savory, Burgundian character over fruit-forward ripeness.

wines.name: Kutch Sonoma Coast Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Delicate and savory with red fruit, dried herbs, and earthy, whole-cluster spice.

wines.name: Kutch McDougall Ranch Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Ethereal and complex with wild strawberry, rose petal, and chalky minerality.

producers.awards: A darling of sommeliers and natural wine enthusiasts; praised for Burgundian restraint.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Rivers-Marie
producers.philosophy: Thomas Rivers Brown and his wife Genevieve Marie produce small-lot wines from exceptional vineyard sites across Sonoma and Napa. The focus is on pure, site-specific expression with minimal winemaking intervention.

wines.name: Rivers-Marie Summa Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Intense and mineral-driven with dark cherry, iron, and savory complexity from an iconic Sonoma Coast site.

wines.name: Rivers-Marie Silver Eagle Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Elegant and perfumed with red fruit, wild herbs, and fine-grained tannins.

producers.awards: Consistently rated 94-98; Thomas Rivers Brown is one of California's most in-demand winemakers.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Hartford Family Winery
producers.philosophy: Don Hartford and his wife Jenny, daughter of Jess Jackson, focus on single-vineyard wines from old vines and cool-climate sites throughout Sonoma. The winery champions the distinct character of each vineyard source.

wines.name: Hartford Court Land's Edge Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Cool-climate elegance with cranberry, wild herb, and bracing coastal acidity.

wines.name: Hartford Fanucchi-Wood Road Zinfandel
wines.notes: Old-vine complexity with blackberry, pepper, and exotic spice.

producers.awards: Consistently rated 90-95 across vineyard designations; excellent value.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Merry Edwards Winery
producers.philosophy: Merry Edwards is a pioneering woman winemaker who has shaped Russian River Valley Pinot Noir for decades. Her wines are known for their rich, expressive style and meticulous vineyard-specific bottlings.

wines.name: Merry Edwards Meredith Estate Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Lush and layered with dark cherry, cola, spice, and plush, velvety texture.

wines.name: Merry Edwards Sauvignon Blanc
wines.notes: Rich and textured with melon, lime, and subtle herbal notes.

producers.awards: One of California's most influential winemakers; Pinot Noirs regularly score 93-96.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: DuMOL
producers.philosophy: DuMOL partners with exceptional vineyard sites across the Russian River Valley and Sonoma Coast to produce wines of refinement and complexity. Winemaker Andy Smith emphasizes whole-cluster fermentation and minimal new oak.

wines.name: DuMOL Ryan Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Elegant and spicy with red cherry, rose petal, and delicate whole-cluster complexity.

wines.name: DuMOL Estate Chardonnay
wines.notes: Precise and mineral with citrus, white flower, and a long, textured finish.

producers.awards: Consistently rated 94-97 across the portfolio; one of Sonoma's most respected producers.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Kosta Browne
producers.philosophy: Founded by Dan Kosta and Michael Browne from a tiny garage operation, Kosta Browne became one of California's most sought-after Pinot Noir producers. The winery sources from premier vineyards across Sonoma and Santa Barbara.

wines.name: Kosta Browne Gap's Crown Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Rich and layered with dark cherry, exotic spice, and a plush, enveloping texture.

wines.name: Kosta Browne Sonoma Coast Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Opulent and fruit-driven with raspberry, cola, and subtle oak spice.

producers.awards: Multiple 97+ scores; one of California's most allocated Pinot Noir labels.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Aubert Wines
producers.philosophy: Mark Aubert, a protege of Helen Turley, produces tiny quantities of Chardonnay and Pinot Noir from premier Sonoma Coast and Napa vineyards. His wines are known for their richness, complexity, and impeccable balance.

wines.name: Aubert Lauren Estate Chardonnay
wines.notes: Opulent and layered with citrus curd, hazelnut, and a long, mineral-inflected finish.

wines.name: Aubert UV-SL Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Dark and concentrated with black cherry, spice, and velvety texture.

producers.awards: Multiple perfect 100-point scores for Chardonnay; one of California's most allocated whites.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Failla
producers.philosophy: Ehren Jordan, former winemaker at Turley and Neyers, founded Failla to pursue his passion for cool-climate wines. The estate sources from extreme Sonoma Coast and Anderson Valley sites to produce wines of finesse and tension.

wines.name: Failla Hirsch Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Mineral and savory with wild cherry, iron, dried herb, and sea-salt inflected acidity.

wines.name: Failla Estate Vineyard Chardonnay
wines.notes: Taut and precise with lemon, wet stone, and a steely mineral backbone.

producers.awards: Consistently rated 93-97; one of Sonoma Coast's most respected producers.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Freeman Vineyard & Winery
producers.philosophy: Ken and Akiko Freeman founded their winery with a vision of producing elegant, terroir-driven wines that bridge California and Burgundy sensibilities. Akiko, originally from Tokyo, brings a refined aesthetic to every aspect of the operation.

wines.name: Freeman Akiko's Cuvee Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Elegant and precise with red cherry, spice, and silky, refined tannins.

wines.name: Freeman Ryo-Fu Chardonnay
wines.notes: Crisp and mineral-driven with citrus, pear, and subtle lees complexity.

producers.awards: First Japanese-American owned Sonoma winery; consistently rated 92-95.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Red Car Wine
producers.philosophy: Red Car focuses exclusively on estate-grown wines from their rugged Sonoma Coast vineyard perched above the Pacific. The estate is farmed biodynamically and yields wines of remarkable tension and coastal character.

wines.name: Red Car Estate Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Taut and mineral with cranberry, sea spray, and a savory, wind-swept quality.

wines.name: Red Car Estate Chardonnay
wines.notes: Lean and focused with green apple, citrus, and chalky minerality.

producers.awards: Praised for its extreme coastal terroir; consistently rated 91-95.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Foxen Winery
producers.philosophy: Bill Wathen and Dick Dore founded Foxen in a rustic blacksmith shop, and the winery retains that no-frills ethos. They produce both Burgundian and Rhone-inspired wines from top Santa Barbara County vineyards.

wines.name: Foxen Bien Nacido Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Vibrant and complex with cherry, wild herb, and earthy undertones from a legendary vineyard.

wines.name: Foxen Williamson-Doré Syrah
wines.notes: Dark and savory with blackberry, smoked meat, and cracked pepper.

producers.awards: A Santa Barbara icon; consistently praised for vineyard-designated wines at fair prices.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Sanford Winery
producers.philosophy: Richard Sanford was a pioneer of Sta. Rita Hills viticulture, proving that world-class Pinot Noir could thrive in this cool, windswept appellation. The estate is now certified biodynamic and continues his legacy of site-driven winemaking.

wines.name: Sanford Sanford & Benedict Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Complex and savory with red cherry, sage, lavender, and stony minerality from the historic site.

wines.name: Sanford Sta. Rita Hills Chardonnay
wines.notes: Crisp and elegant with citrus, white flower, and bright acidity.

producers.awards: Sanford & Benedict is one of California's most historic Pinot Noir vineyards.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Melville Winery
producers.philosophy: Chad Melville produces cool-climate wines of remarkable precision and tension from the family's estate vineyard in the heart of Sta. Rita Hills. The wines emphasize savory, mineral-driven character over fruit-forward ripeness.

wines.name: Melville Estate Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Taut and savory with cranberry, dried herb, iron, and bracing acidity.

wines.name: Melville Estate Syrah
wines.notes: Cool-climate elegance with dark fruit, olive, white pepper, and a savory, Northern Rhone quality.

producers.awards: One of Sta. Rita Hills' most respected estate producers; consistently rated 93-96.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: The Ojai Vineyard
producers.philosophy: Adam Tolmach, a founder of Au Bon Climat, launched Ojai Vineyard to pursue his passion for Rhone varieties and cool-climate wines. The winery is known for a natural, low-intervention approach and exceptional vineyard sourcing.

wines.name: Ojai Vineyard Bien Nacido Syrah
wines.notes: Savory and complex with dark fruit, olive, cured meat, and peppery spice.

wines.name: Ojai Vineyard Roll Ranch Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Delicate and earthy with red cherry, dried herb, and gentle tannins.

producers.awards: A Santa Barbara original and sommelier favorite; consistently praised for authenticity.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Epoch Estate Wines
producers.philosophy: Jordan Fiorentini produces extraordinary Rhone and Spanish-inspired wines from a 100-year-old vineyard on pure calcareous soils in the Adelaida District. The estate combines old-vine heritage with precise, site-driven winemaking.

wines.name: Epoch Paderewski Vineyard Syrah
wines.notes: Rich and mineral with dark fruit, crushed limestone, and savory spice from ancient soils.

wines.name: Epoch Ingenuity
wines.notes: A vibrant Rhone-style blend with raspberry, garrigue, and silky texture.

producers.awards: Rapidly ascending; praised by Jeb Dunnuck and Antonio Galloni with scores up to 99 points.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Turley Wine Cellars
producers.philosophy: Larry Turley, co-founder of Frog's Leap, devoted his second act to preserving California's old-vine heritage vineyards. Turley produces an extraordinary range of single-vineyard Zinfandels from ancient vines across California.

wines.name: Turley Pesenti Vineyard Zinfandel
wines.notes: Rich and complex from centenarian vines with blackberry, earth, spice, and remarkable depth.

wines.name: Turley Dusi Vineyard Zinfandel
wines.notes: Concentrated and structured with dark fruit, pepper, and the unmistakable character of old Paso vines.

producers.awards: One of California's most important old-vine champions; consistently rated 93-98.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.sommelierNote: Larry Turley farms over 50 old-vine vineyards across California — some with pre-Prohibition vines. Powerful, honest, no-makeup wines.

producers.name: Adelaida Vineyards
producers.philosophy: Adelaida farms organically at high elevation on limestone soils in the Adelaida District, producing wines that rival cooler climate regions in elegance. The estate is a champion of the western Paso Robles terroir and its distinctive calcareous character.

wines.name: Adelaida HMR Estate Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Elegant and mineral-driven with red cherry, dried herb, and chalky limestone character.

wines.name: Adelaida Viking Estate Syrah
wines.notes: Dark and structured with blackberry, olive, and savory spice.

producers.awards: HMR is one of Paso Robles' most historic vineyards; wines consistently rated 91-95.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Domaine Serene
producers.philosophy: Ken and Grace Evenstad built Domaine Serene into one of Oregon's most prestigious estates through relentless investment in vineyard and winery. The estate produces powerful, concentrated Pinot Noirs that rival Burgundy Grand Crus.

wines.name: Domaine Serene Evenstad Reserve Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Rich and concentrated with dark cherry, spice, earth, and refined tannins.

wines.name: Domaine Serene Evenstad Reserve Chardonnay
wines.notes: Opulent and layered with citrus, hazelnut, and creamy, lingering texture.

producers.awards: Beat Burgundy Grand Crus in blind tastings; one of Oregon's most acclaimed estates.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Archery Summit
producers.philosophy: Archery Summit was founded with the singular goal of producing world-class Pinot Noir from the red volcanic Jory soils of the Dundee Hills. The estate farms sustainably and produces multiple vineyard-designated bottlings.

wines.name: Archery Summit Premier Cuvée Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Layered and complex with dark cherry, baking spice, and a long, velvety finish.

wines.name: Archery Summit Arcus Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Concentrated and structured with dark fruit, earth, and firm Dundee Hills tannins.

producers.awards: One of the Dundee Hills' original prestige estates; consistently rated 91-95.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Brick House Vineyards
producers.philosophy: Doug Tunnell, a former CBS News correspondent, farms his Ribbon Ridge estate with certified organic and biodynamic methods. The wines are crafted naturally with native yeast and minimal sulfur additions.

wines.name: Brick House Les Dijonnais Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Pure and elegant with red cherry, wild herb, and a long, mineral-driven finish.

wines.name: Brick House Evelyn's Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Rich and layered with dark fruit, spice, and silky, concentrated texture.

producers.awards: One of Oregon's most committed biodynamic producers; consistently rated 91-95.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Patricia Green Cellars
producers.philosophy: The late Patty Green was a beloved figure in Oregon wine, producing an extraordinary range of vineyard-designated Pinot Noirs at remarkable value. Her team continues the legacy of honest, terroir-driven winemaking.

wines.name: Patricia Green Balcombe Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Elegant and complex with red cherry, rose petal, and silky Ribbon Ridge texture.

wines.name: Patricia Green Estate Old Vine Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Concentrated and savory with dark fruit, forest floor, and fine-grained tannins.

producers.awards: A beloved Oregon producer; extraordinary quality-to-price ratio across 20+ vineyard bottlings.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Lingua Franca
producers.philosophy: Founded by master sommelier Larry Stone with winemaker Dominique Lafon of Burgundy's Comtes Lafon, Lingua Franca bridges Oregon and Burgundy. The estate is farmed biodynamically and produces wines of remarkable finesse.

wines.name: Lingua Franca Avni Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Elegant and taut with red cherry, savory herb, and precise, mineral-driven structure.

wines.name: Lingua Franca Estate Chardonnay
wines.notes: Burgundian in character with citrus, white flower, and a long, mineral finish.

producers.awards: One of Oregon's most exciting new estates; praised for its Burgundian precision.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.sommelierNote: Larry Stone MS teamed with Dominique Lafon — Burgundian precision from the Eola-Amity Hills, Oregon's most exciting sub-AVA.

producers.name: Walter Scott
producers.philosophy: Ken Pahlow and Erica Landon produce terroir-focused wines from exceptional Eola-Amity Hills vineyards with a minimal-intervention approach. The wines are prized for their purity, tension, and sense of place.

wines.name: Walter Scott Freedom Hill Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Complex and savory with dark cherry, crushed herb, and firm, mineral-laden structure.

wines.name: Walter Scott Cuvée Anne Chardonnay
wines.notes: Taut and precise with citrus, wet stone, and racy acidity.

producers.awards: A sommelier cult favorite; rapidly ascending among Oregon's most sought-after producers.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Kelley Fox Wines
producers.philosophy: Kelley Fox produces profoundly terroir-driven Pinot Noir with zero additions, using only native yeast and no sulfur at bottling. Her wines from Maresh and Weber vineyards are among Oregon's most singular expressions.

wines.name: Kelley Fox Maresh Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Ethereal and complex with wild strawberry, dried rose, and extraordinary transparency of site.

wines.name: Kelley Fox Weber Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Savory and earthy with red cherry, mushroom, and fine-grained, natural texture.

producers.awards: A natural wine pioneer in Oregon; praised for radical transparency and terroir purity.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Gran Moraine
producers.philosophy: Planted on a high-elevation site with deep marine sedimentary soils, Gran Moraine is a newer estate focused on single-vineyard wines of precision and structure. Winemaker Shane Moore emphasizes site expression and minimal intervention.

wines.name: Gran Moraine Estate Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Structured and mineral with dark cherry, crushed stone, and fine, chalky tannins.

wines.name: Gran Moraine Estate Chardonnay
wines.notes: Precise and lean with citrus, green apple, and vibrant acidity.

producers.awards: Quickly gaining recognition; rated 92-95 from early vintages.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Résonance
producers.philosophy: Louis Jadot's Oregon project, Résonance, brought Burgundian expertise to the Willamette Valley. Winemaker Jacques Lardière applies his decades of Burgundy experience to Oregon terroir with remarkable results.

wines.name: Résonance Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Burgundian in character with red cherry, earth, spice, and silky, refined texture.

wines.name: Résonance Découverte Vineyard Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Elegant and complex with wild strawberry, dried herb, and fine-grained tannins.

producers.awards: Louis Jadot's Oregon estate; consistently rated 93-96.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Antica Terra
producers.philosophy: Maggie Harrison, formerly of Sine Qua Non, produces hauntingly beautiful Pinot Noirs from ancient volcanic and marine sedimentary soils in the Eola-Amity Hills. The wines are crafted with an artist's sensibility and radical attention to detail.

wines.name: Antica Terra Botanica Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Ethereal and complex with wild strawberry, dried flowers, earth, and an almost transparent mineral purity.

wines.name: Antica Terra Aurata Chardonnay
wines.notes: Precise and luminous with citrus oil, crushed shell, and vibrant acidity.

producers.awards: One of Oregon's most sought-after producers; praised for transcendent, singular wines.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.sommelierNote: Maggie Harrison's deeply personal project — each bottling (Botanica, Ceras, Antikythera) is a distinct artistic expression of Willamette Pinot.

producers.name: Columbia Crest
producers.philosophy: Columbia Crest produces exceptional value wines from Washington's Horse Heaven Hills, proving that world-class wine doesn't require a luxury price tag. The winery's H3 and Grand Estates tiers offer remarkable quality for the price.

wines.name: Columbia Crest Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Polished and complex with dark fruit, cedar, and firm structure at a fraction of Napa pricing.

wines.name: Columbia Crest H3 Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Generous and fruit-driven with blackberry, chocolate, and soft tannins.

producers.awards: Walter Clore Wine of the Year winner; one of the best values in American wine.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: L'Ecole No 41
producers.philosophy: Housed in a historic Frenchtown schoolhouse, L'Ecole is one of Walla Walla's founding wineries. The Ferguson family produces estate wines from sustainably farmed vineyards across Walla Walla and the Columbia Valley.

wines.name: L'Ecole Ferguson Vineyard
wines.notes: A Bordeaux-style blend with dark fruit, graphite, cedar, and structured, age-worthy tannins.

wines.name: L'Ecole Semillon
wines.notes: Crisp and aromatic with citrus, melon, and subtle waxy texture.

producers.awards: One of Walla Walla's founding wineries; Ferguson bottling consistently rated 92-96.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Betz Family Winery
producers.philosophy: Bob Betz, a Master of Wine and former VP at Chateau Ste. Michelle, founded his boutique winery to produce wines that honor both Bordeaux and Rhone traditions. His wines are crafted with deep knowledge and restraint.

wines.name: Betz Family Père de Famille Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Structured and complex with cassis, tobacco, graphite, and firm, classically styled tannins.

wines.name: Betz Family La Serenne Syrah
wines.notes: Northern Rhone-inspired with dark fruit, smoked meat, white pepper, and stony minerality.

producers.awards: One of Washington's most respected producers; consistently rated 94-97.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Long Shadows Vintners
producers.philosophy: Allen Shoup's visionary project pairs world-renowned winemakers with Washington State fruit, producing a portfolio of wines that showcase international expertise. Each wine is crafted by a different celebrated winemaker.

wines.name: Long Shadows Feather Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Made by Randy Dunn; powerful and structured with cassis, sage, and firm mountain-style tannins.

wines.name: Long Shadows Pirouette
wines.notes: Made by Agustin Huneeus; a refined Bordeaux blend with dark fruit, cedar, and elegance.

producers.awards: A unique model in American wine; individual bottlings regularly score 93-97.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Woodward Canyon Winery
producers.philosophy: Rick Small founded Woodward Canyon as one of Walla Walla's original wineries, producing estate wines from some of the region's oldest vines. The winery has been a consistent benchmark for Washington Cabernet for over four decades.

wines.name: Woodward Canyon Old Vines Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Rich and structured with dark fruit, cedar, tobacco, and firm, age-worthy tannins.

wines.name: Woodward Canyon Estate Reserve
wines.notes: Concentrated and complex from the best estate blocks with black fruit, graphite, and polished structure.

producers.awards: One of Walla Walla's founding three wineries; consistently rated 93-97.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Spring Valley Vineyard
producers.philosophy: Farmed by the Corkrum Derby family since 1910, Spring Valley Vineyard produces estate wines from some of Walla Walla's oldest plantings. Each wine is named for a family member and honors generations of agricultural heritage.

wines.name: Spring Valley Uriah
wines.notes: A rich Bordeaux-style blend with plum, dark cherry, chocolate, and velvety tannins.

wines.name: Spring Valley Frederick
wines.notes: Structured and complex with cassis, sage, and firm, age-worthy tannins from historic vines.

producers.awards: Deeply rooted in Walla Walla history; wines consistently rated 91-95.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Mark Ryan Winery
producers.philosophy: Mark Ryan McNeilly started as a garagiste winemaker and grew his label into one of Washington's most acclaimed producers. The wines are bold, generous, and crafted to showcase Washington's best vineyard sites.

wines.name: Mark Ryan The Dissident
wines.notes: A powerful Bordeaux blend with cassis, dark chocolate, espresso, and ripe, structured tannins.

wines.name: Mark Ryan Dead Horse
wines.notes: Rich and opulent from Ciel du Cheval Vineyard with dark fruit, mocha, and velvet texture.

producers.awards: Consistently rated 92-96; one of Washington's best-value premium producers.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Avennia
producers.philosophy: Chris Peterson, former assistant at DeLille Cellars, produces both Bordeaux and Rhone-inspired wines from Washington's top vineyards. His focus is on balanced, food-friendly wines that showcase Washington's diverse terroir.

wines.name: Avennia Gravura
wines.notes: A polished Bordeaux blend with dark fruit, graphite, herbs, and structured tannins.

wines.name: Avennia Oliane
wines.notes: Northern Rhone-inspired with dark fruit, olive, smoked meat, and peppery spice.

producers.awards: Named a Wine & Spirits Top 100 Winery multiple times; consistently rated 92-96.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Pursued by Bear
producers.philosophy: Actor Kyle MacLachlan's Washington wine project, produced in collaboration with Dunham Cellars, draws on the state's finest vineyards. The name references Shakespeare's famous stage direction from The Winter's Tale.

wines.name: Pursued by Bear Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Rich and concentrated with cassis, dark chocolate, and supple, polished tannins.

wines.name: Baby Bear Syrah
wines.notes: A fruit-driven Syrah with dark berry, pepper, and approachable, smooth texture.

producers.awards: Consistently well-reviewed; a serious wine project beyond celebrity branding.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Dr. Konstantin Frank
producers.philosophy: Dr. Frank, a Ukrainian immigrant, proved that European vinifera grapes could thrive in the Finger Lakes, revolutionizing Eastern American viticulture. His descendants continue to push boundaries with an extraordinary range of varieties.

wines.name: Dr. Konstantin Frank Dry Riesling
wines.notes: Crisp and aromatic with green apple, citrus, and racy Finger Lakes acidity.

wines.name: Dr. Konstantin Frank Rkatsiteli
wines.notes: A rare Georgian variety with stone fruit, honey, and bright, tangy acidity.

producers.awards: The father of Eastern American vinifera winemaking; a living monument to viticultural innovation.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Wölffer Estate
producers.philosophy: Wölffer Estate has become a Hamptons institution, producing sophisticated maritime-influenced wines and one of America's most popular rosés. The estate's Long Island vineyard benefits from the moderating influence of the Atlantic Ocean.

wines.name: Wölffer Estate Selection Merlot
wines.notes: Elegant and medium-bodied with red plum, herbs, and gentle tannins shaped by maritime climate.

wines.name: Wölffer Estate Rosé
wines.notes: Pale and refreshing with strawberry, citrus, and crisp, dry minerality.

producers.awards: One of the most recognized East Coast wine estates; rosé is a summer icon.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Barboursville Vineyards
producers.philosophy: Founded by the Zonin family of Italy on the former plantation of Governor James Barbour, Barboursville is Virginia's most acclaimed winery. Italian winemaker Luca Paschina has spent decades proving Virginia's potential for world-class wine.

wines.name: Barboursville Octagon
wines.notes: Virginia's flagship Bordeaux blend with dark fruit, tobacco, and firm, elegant tannins.

wines.name: Barboursville Nebbiolo Reserve
wines.notes: Remarkable for Virginia with cherry, tar, rose petal, and firm tannins.

producers.awards: Octagon is Virginia's most acclaimed wine; Barboursville put Virginia on the wine map.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: RdV Vineyards
producers.philosophy: Rutger de Vink established RdV with the singular goal of producing Bordeaux-class wines in Virginia, enlisting top Bordeaux consultants Eric Boissenot and Derenoncourt. The estate farms meticulously in the foothills of the Blue Ridge Mountains.

wines.name: RdV Lost Mountain
wines.notes: A Left Bank-inspired blend with dark fruit, graphite, cedar, and structured, age-worthy tannins.

wines.name: RdV Rendezvous
wines.notes: A Right Bank-styled blend with plum, violets, and silky, refined texture.

producers.awards: Widely considered Virginia's finest red wine producer; scores reaching 95+ from top critics.

buyingGuide.note: Often sold through mailing lists or allocated to restaurants. Check Wine Searcher or the winery website directly.

producers.name: Bass Phillip
producers.philosophy: Bass Phillip is one of Australia's most revered Pinot Noir producers, practicing biodynamic viticulture across its low-yielding Gippsland vineyards. The estate pursues Burgundian elegance with meticulous hand-harvesting and minimal intervention in the cellar.

wines.name: Premium Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Intense and silky with layers of dark cherry, truffle, and forest floor complexity.

wines.name: Estate Chardonnay
wines.notes: Refined and mineral-driven with citrus blossom and subtle oak integration.

producers.awards: Consistently rated among Australia's finest Pinot Noirs by Halliday and Langton's Classification.

buyingGuide.note: Limited production. Check Wine Searcher for current availability, or ask your local fine wine shop.

producers.sommelierNote: Phillip Jones's Premium Pinot Noir from ultra-low-yielding vines is among Australia's most intense and age-worthy Pinots.

producers.name: Mount Mary
producers.philosophy: Mount Mary is a benchmark Yarra Valley estate focused on elegant Bordeaux-style blends and pristine varietal wines. The winery champions restraint and balance, allowing the cool-climate terroir to speak through each vintage.

wines.name: Quintet
wines.notes: A refined Bordeaux blend with blackcurrant, cedar, and fine-grained tannins.

wines.name: Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Perfumed and graceful with red cherry, spice, and silky texture.

producers.awards: Langton's Classification of Distinguished Australian Wine — Exceptional tier.

buyingGuide.note: Limited production. Check Wine Searcher for current availability, or ask your local fine wine shop.

producers.sommelierNote: One of the Yarra's founding estates — Sam Middleton continues John Middleton's tradition of elegant, understated wines.

producers.name: Cullen
producers.philosophy: Cullen is a pioneering biodynamic estate in Margaret River, led by Vanya Cullen with a deep commitment to sustainability. The wines reflect a seamless union of power and finesse shaped by the unique Wilyabrup terroir.

wines.name: Diana Madeline
wines.notes: A Bordeaux blend of immense depth with cassis, graphite, and integrated oak.

wines.name: Kevin John Chardonnay
wines.notes: Rich yet poised with white peach, cashew, and a mineral finish.

producers.awards: Multiple James Halliday Winery of the Year accolades.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Grosset
producers.philosophy: Jeffrey Grosset is regarded as Australia's foremost Riesling producer, crafting age-worthy wines from the Clare Valley's distinctive terroir. Organic practices and screw-cap advocacy underscore a relentless pursuit of purity.

wines.name: Polish Hill Riesling
wines.notes: Taut and minerally with lime zest and steely acidity that develops beautifully with age.

wines.name: Springvale Riesling
wines.notes: Floral and approachable with citrus and green apple freshness.

producers.awards: Widely recognized as Australia's greatest Riesling producer by international critics.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Hentley Farm
producers.philosophy: Hentley Farm produces intensely flavored wines from a single estate on the western ridge of the Barossa Valley. The winery focuses on small-batch, site-driven winemaking that captures the power of old-vine Barossa fruit.

wines.name: The Beast Shiraz
wines.notes: Dense and opulent with blackberry, dark chocolate, and smoky oak.

wines.name: The Beauty Shiraz
wines.notes: Elegant and perfumed with violet, plum, and silky tannins.

producers.awards: Multiple trophies at Royal Adelaide Wine Show and high Parker scores.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Two Hands
producers.philosophy: Two Hands sources premium fruit from Australia's finest regions to craft bold, expressive Shiraz. The winemaking approach is hands-off, allowing each vineyard's character to dominate without excessive oak influence.

wines.name: Bella's Garden Shiraz
wines.notes: Rich and layered with dark fruit, licorice, and a velvety finish.

wines.name: Gnarly Dudes Shiraz
wines.notes: Approachable and juicy with plum, spice, and soft tannins from old-vine Barossa fruit.

producers.awards: Named in Wine Spectator's Top 100 wines multiple times.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.sommelierNote: Michael Twelftree sources from exceptional vineyards across Australia — garden series, picture series, and flagship wines.

producers.name: Noon
producers.philosophy: Noon Winery is a tiny, family-run estate producing some of McLaren Vale's most concentrated and soulful wines. Drew Noon MW crafts small quantities with minimal intervention, resulting in wines of remarkable purity.

wines.name: Reserve Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Powerful and structured with blackcurrant, mint, and firm tannins.

wines.name: Eclipse
wines.notes: A lush Grenache-Shiraz blend with raspberry, spice, and velvety texture.

producers.awards: Langton's Classification of Distinguished Australian Wine — Outstanding tier.

buyingGuide.note: Limited production. Check Wine Searcher for current availability, or ask your local fine wine shop.

producers.name: Tahbilk
producers.philosophy: Tahbilk is one of Australia's oldest family-owned wineries, custodians of heritage vineyards including the world's largest single holding of Marsanne. Sustainable practices and respect for tradition guide every vintage.

wines.name: 1860 Vines Shiraz
wines.notes: Sourced from pre-phylloxera vines with remarkable depth, earth, and spice complexity.

wines.name: Marsanne
wines.notes: Honeyed and textured with quince, almond, and waxy richness that rewards cellaring.

producers.awards: James Halliday five-star winery; heritage vineyard recognized internationally.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Grant Burge
producers.philosophy: Grant Burge is a fifth-generation Barossa family producing richly flavored wines from some of the region's oldest vineyards. The range spans accessible everyday wines to single-vineyard icons.

wines.name: Meshach Shiraz
wines.notes: A flagship old-vine Shiraz of great intensity with dark fruit, mocha, and spice.

wines.name: Benchmark Chardonnay
wines.notes: Fresh and easy-drinking with tropical fruit and gentle oak.

producers.awards: Meshach Shiraz listed in Langton's Classification of Distinguished Australian Wine.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Peter Lehmann
producers.philosophy: Peter Lehmann was the champion of Barossa growers, establishing a winery to purchase fruit when large companies abandoned the region. The legacy lives on through generous, fruit-driven wines that embody the spirit of the Barossa.

wines.name: Stonewell Shiraz
wines.notes: Full-bodied and complex with blackberry, dark chocolate, and cedar.

wines.name: Wigan Riesling
wines.notes: Vibrant and age-worthy with lime, toast, and keen minerality.

producers.awards: Stonewell Shiraz is a Langton's classified wine with consistent critical acclaim.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Rockford
producers.philosophy: Rockford is a Barossa institution that uses traditional equipment including a century-old basket press to craft characterful wines. Robert O'Callaghan sources from old-vine growers to preserve the region's viticultural heritage.

wines.name: Basket Press Shiraz
wines.notes: A Barossa classic with dark fruit, earth, and structured tannins from old vines.

wines.name: Black Shiraz Sparkling
wines.notes: Rich and celebratory with berry compote, chocolate, and persistent mousse.

producers.awards: Basket Press Shiraz is Langton's classified and a perennial critics' favourite.

buyingGuide.note: Limited production. Check Wine Searcher for current availability, or ask your local fine wine shop.

producers.name: Turkey Flat
producers.philosophy: Turkey Flat is custodian of some of the Barossa's oldest Shiraz vines, planted in 1847. The Schulz family combines heritage viticulture with a fresh, contemporary winemaking approach.

wines.name: Butchers Block Red
wines.notes: A juicy GSM blend with red berry, spice, and easy-drinking charm.

wines.name: Shiraz
wines.notes: Generous and warm with plum, licorice, and polished tannins from ancient vines.

producers.awards: James Halliday five-star winery with consistent high ratings.

buyingGuide.note: Widely available at major wine retailers.

producers.name: Mitolo
producers.philosophy: Mitolo produces bold, site-expressive wines from McLaren Vale with winemaker Ben Glaetzer at the helm. The estate focuses on powerful, contemporary-styled reds with meticulous fruit selection.

wines.name: Savitar Shiraz
wines.notes: Opulent and layered with blackberry, dark chocolate, and vanilla spice.

wines.name: Serpico Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Firm and structured with cassis, olive, and cedar complexity.

producers.awards: Multiple trophies at major Australian wine shows.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Howard Park
producers.philosophy: Howard Park draws on the best of two Western Australian regions, Great Southern and Margaret River, to produce refined, terroir-driven wines. The Burch family is committed to quality across all tiers from accessible to iconic.

wines.name: Abercrombie Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Structured and elegant with dark berry, graphite, and fine tannin architecture.

wines.name: Porongurup Riesling
wines.notes: Taut and pure with lime juice, floral notes, and a long mineral finish.

producers.awards: Abercrombie Cabernet is a Langton's classified wine; five-star Halliday winery.

buyingGuide.note: Available at specialty wine shops and online retailers, though not always in stock.

producers.name: Larry Cherubino
