wines.name: Domaine de Baal Red
wines.notes: Elegant and structured with dark fruit, graphite, and a long, mineral finish.

wines.name: Domaine de Baal Blanc
wines.notes: Aromatic and fresh with white peach, honeysuckle, and a crisp, clean finish.

producers.awards: Praised by Jancis Robinson as one of Lebanon's finest boutique producers.

producers.name: Flam Winery
producers.philosophy: Brothers Golan and Gilad Flam produce some of Israel's most refined wines from the cool Judean Hills. Their Burgundy-trained sensibility yields wines of elegance and restraint in a country known for power.

wines.name: Flam Noble
wines.notes: Israel's benchmark Bordeaux blend — cassis, cedar, and graphite with a long, structured finish.

wines.name: Flam Classico White
wines.notes: Fresh and aromatic with citrus, passion fruit, and a crisp, clean finish.

producers.awards: Consistently top-rated Israeli winery; Wine Advocate 90+ scores.

producers.name: Galil Mountain
producers.philosophy: A joint venture between Golan Heights Winery and Kibbutz Yiron, Galil Mountain benefits from cool, high-altitude vineyards in the Upper Galilee. The wines offer excellent quality at accessible prices.

wines.name: Galil Mountain Yiron
wines.notes: Rich and complex with dark fruit, oak spice, and a smooth, polished tannic finish.

wines.name: Galil Mountain Viognier
wines.notes: Fragrant and golden with apricot, honeysuckle, and a lush, textured palate.

producers.awards: Wine Enthusiast 90+ points; excellent value from Israel.

producers.name: Yatir Winery
producers.philosophy: Yatir's vineyards sit at the edge of the Negev desert, at 900 meters elevation near the ancient Yatir Forest. The extreme desert-meets-forest terroir produces deeply concentrated, powerful wines unique in the world.

wines.name: Yatir Forest
wines.notes: Deeply concentrated with blackcurrant, dark chocolate, and a powerful, velvety tannic frame.

wines.name: Yatir Creek
wines.notes: Intense and inky with violet, blackberry, and a structured, age-worthy finish.

producers.awards: Wine Advocate 93+ points for Yatir Forest; a collaboration with Carmel Winery.

producers.name: Okro's Wines
producers.philosophy: John Okruashvili produces natural wines using traditional Georgian qvevri (clay amphorae) buried underground. His amber wines from Rkatsiteli are textbook examples of Georgia's ancient winemaking tradition.

wines.name: Okro's Rkatsiteli
wines.notes: Deep amber with dried apricot, walnut, tea, and a tannic, complex structure from qvevri aging.

wines.name: Okro's Saperavi
wines.notes: Intensely dark and powerful with black plum, chocolate, and earthy, gripping tannins.

producers.awards: Featured in natural wine guides; praised by Alice Feiring and Isabelle Legeron MW.

producers.name: Lapati Wines
producers.philosophy: Lapati produces small-batch qvevri wines from Kvareli in the heart of Kakheti. The estate honors Georgia's 8,000-year winemaking tradition while bringing a modern sensibility to cleanliness and precision.

wines.name: Lapati Kisi Qvevri
wines.notes: Amber and aromatic with dried herb, quince, and a tannic, textured mouthfeel.

wines.name: Lapati Saperavi
wines.notes: Deep and brooding with black cherry, spice, and a dense, chewy tannic structure.

producers.awards: Growing recognition in the natural wine world.

producers.name: Iago's Wine
producers.philosophy: Iago Bitarishvili is one of Georgia's natural wine pioneers, producing just two wines — a qvevri Chinuri and a Tavkveri rosé — from his small family vineyard. His Chinuri is considered the benchmark for this rare variety.

wines.name: Iago's Chinuri
wines.notes: Golden and complex with apple, herb, honeycomb, and a gentle tannic grip from qvevri maceration.

wines.name: Iago's Tavkveri
wines.notes: Pale rosé with wild strawberry, herb, and a fresh, crunchy, easy-drinking character.

producers.awards: One of Georgia's most famous natural winemakers; championed by Alice Feiring.

producers.name: Domaine Jean-René Germanier
producers.philosophy: Gilles Besse and the Germanier family produce benchmark Valais wines, particularly the rare Amigne grape for which they are Switzerland's reference producer. Their Syrah Calinifer rivals the best of the northern Rhône.

wines.name: Amigne de Vétroz Grand Cru
wines.notes: Unique and honeyed with quince, acacia, and a rich, waxy texture balanced by bright acidity.

wines.name: Calinifer Syrah
wines.notes: Peppery and refined with dark fruit, olive, and a northern Rhône-like elegance.

producers.awards: Switzerland's reference Amigne producer; multiple Grand Prix du Vin Suisse awards.

producers.name: Cave du Rhodan
producers.philosophy: Olivier Mounir leads this cooperative-turned-boutique estate in Salgesch, the capital of Swiss Pinot Noir. Their wines combine Alpine freshness with surprising depth and structure.

wines.name: Salquenen Grand Cru Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Elegant and perfumed with raspberry, spice, and a silky, cool-climate finesse.

wines.name: Cayas Syrah
wines.notes: Dark and peppery with blackberry, violet, and a polished, medium-bodied palate.

producers.awards: Grand Prix du Vin Suisse medals; leading Salgesch estate.

producers.name: Gantenbein
producers.philosophy: Daniel and Martha Gantenbein produce Switzerland's most celebrated Pinot Noir from steep, terraced vineyards in the Bündner Herrschaft. Inspired by Burgundy, their wines are allocated and virtually impossible to find.

wines.name: Gantenbein Pinot Noir
wines.notes: Exquisite and Burgundian with dark cherry, earth, spice, and a seamless, silky texture.

wines.name: Gantenbein Chardonnay
wines.notes: Rich and layered with citrus, hazelnut, and a toasty, persistent finish.

producers.awards: Switzerland's most famous wine; consistently compared to Grand Cru Burgundy.

producers.name: Kayra
producers.philosophy: Kayra is a pioneer in rediscovering Turkey's indigenous grape varieties, working with internationally trained winemakers to reveal their potential. The estate champions Anatolian grapes like Öküzgözü and Boğazkere with modern winemaking techniques.

wines.name: Kayra Vintage Öküzgözü
wines.notes: Aromatic and medium-bodied with sour cherry, dried herb, and a soft, elegant finish.

wines.name: Kayra Imperial Boğazkere
wines.notes: Dense and tannic with dark plum, leather, and a powerful, age-worthy structure.

producers.awards: Decanter World Wine Awards gold; Turkey's most awarded winery for indigenous varieties.

producers.name: Corvus
producers.philosophy: Founded by gastronome Reha Öğünlü in Turkish Thrace, Corvus produces refined, terroir-driven wines from international varieties grown in the region's continental climate. Quality is uncompromising and production is small.

wines.name: Corvus Corpus Red
wines.notes: Elegant and herbal with red fruit, bell pepper, and a fine, structured finish.

wines.name: Corvus Sauvignon Blanc
wines.notes: Crisp and aromatic with citrus, green herb, and a mineral-tinged, refreshing palate.

producers.awards: Recognized as one of Turkey's finest boutique wineries by Decanter.

producers.name: Movia
producers.philosophy: Aleš Kristančič runs this iconic biodynamic estate straddling the Slovenian-Italian border. His Lunar bottling, disgorged by the buyer, and extended-maceration Rebula are among Central Europe's most exciting and original wines.

wines.name: Movia Lunar
wines.notes: Unfiltered and cloudy with apple, bread dough, and a wild, textured character — disgorged by the drinker.

wines.name: Movia Veliko Bianco
wines.notes: Deep gold with dried fruit, honey, and herb from extended skin maceration in a complex, powerful white.

producers.awards: One of Central Europe's most famous natural wine estates; biodynamic pioneer.

producers.name: Kabaj
producers.philosophy: Jean-Michel Morel, a Burgundy-trained Frenchman, and his Slovenian wife Katja produce skin-contact wines of remarkable depth in Goriška Brda. Their extended-maceration whites are a bridge between Friulian and Slovenian traditions.

wines.name: Kabaj Rebula
wines.notes: Amber-hued and complex with dried apricot, walnut, and a grippy, tannic structure.

wines.name: Kabaj Amfora Malvazija
wines.notes: Deep and textured from amphora aging with quince, herb, and a saline, persistent finish.

producers.awards: Featured in Simon Woolf's Amber Revolution; praised by Jancis Robinson.

producers.name: Monte Xanic
producers.philosophy: Monte Xanic was one of the first boutique wineries in Mexico's Valle de Guadalupe, helping establish the region as Latin America's most exciting emerging wine destination. The estate produces polished, internationally styled wines.

wines.name: Monte Xanic Gran Ricardo
wines.notes: Mexico's flagship Bordeaux blend — cassis, oak spice, and a dense, structured, age-worthy palate.

wines.name: Monte Xanic Chenin Blanc Viña Kristel
wines.notes: Fresh and tropical with melon, guava, and a crisp, clean finish.

producers.awards: Mexico's most internationally recognized winery; pioneer of Valle de Guadalupe.

producers.name: L.A. Cetto
producers.philosophy: L.A. Cetto is Mexico's largest premium winery, founded by Italian immigrants who brought Nebbiolo to Baja California. Their Nebbiolo Reserva is unique in the Americas and the estate has introduced millions to Mexican wine.

wines.name: L.A. Cetto Nebbiolo Reserva
wines.notes: One of the few Nebbiolos outside Italy — tar, rose, cherry, and a firm tannic structure.

wines.name: L.A. Cetto Petite Sirah Reserva
wines.notes: Dense and inky with blackberry, chocolate, and a powerful, full-bodied finish.

producers.awards: Mexico's most-awarded winery internationally; over 500 global medals.

producers.name: Miolo
producers.philosophy: Miolo is one of Brazil's largest and most ambitious quality producers, with vineyards spanning from the Serra Gaúcha hills to the emerging Campanha region. Their collaboration with Michel Rolland produced the flagship Lote 43.

wines.name: Miolo Lote 43
wines.notes: Brazil's flagship Bordeaux blend — dark fruit, vanilla, and a polished, concentrated finish.

wines.name: Miolo Seival Tannat
wines.notes: Dense and structured with blackberry, spice, and firm, chewy tannins.

producers.awards: Decanter World Wine Awards gold; Michel Rolland consulting on top cuvée.

producers.name: Casa Valduga
producers.philosophy: The Valduga family has been growing grapes in the Serra Gaúcha for generations, producing Brazil's most awarded sparkling wines and still reds. Their commitment to quality has helped define Brazilian wine on the world stage.

wines.name: Casa Valduga 130 Brut
wines.notes: Brazil's benchmark sparkling wine with green apple, toast, and a fine, persistent mousse.

wines.name: Casa Valduga Premium Cabernet Sauvignon
wines.notes: Ripe and smooth with blackcurrant, vanilla, and a round, medium-bodied finish.

producers.awards: Brazil's most awarded winery; multiple international sparkling wine trophies.
